content.sanskrit:
arcye viṣṇau śilā-dhīr guruṣu nara-matir vaiṣṇave jāti-buddhir
viṣṇor vā vaiṣṇavānāṁ kali-mala-mathane pāda-tīrthe 'mbu-buddhiḥ
śrī-viṣṇor nāmni mantre sakala-kaluṣa-he śabda-sāmānya-buddhir
viṣṇau sarveśvareśe tad-itara-sama-dhīr yasya vā nārakī saḥ
content.translation: One who thinks the Deity in the temple to be made of wood or stone, who thinks of the spiritual master in the disciplic succession as an ordinary man, who thinks the Vaiṣṇava in the Acyuta-gotra to belong to a certain caste or creed or who thinks of caraṇāmṛta or Ganges water as ordinary water is taken to be a resident of hell. (Padma Purāṇa)

content.sanskrit:
vande 'haṁ śrī-guroḥ śrī-yuta-pada-kamalaṁ śrī-gurūn vaiṣṇavāṁś ca
śrī-rūpaṁ sāgrajātaṁ saha-gaṇa-raghunāthānvitaṁ taṁ sa-jīvam
sādvaitaṁ sāvadhūtaṁ parijana-sahitaṁ kṛṣṇa-caitanya-devaṁ
śrī-rādhā-kṛṣṇa-pādān saha-gaṇa-lalitā-śrī-viśākhānvitāṁś ca
content.translation: I offer praṇāma unto the lotus feet of my [śikṣā and dīkṣā] gurus, my entire guru-varga, and to all Vaiṣṇavas, to Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī, his elder brother Śrīla Sanātana Gosvāmī, to Śrīla Raghunātha dāsa Gosvāmī, Śrīla Jīva Gosvāmī, and all their associates, to Śrī Advaita Ācārya, the avadhūta Nityānanda Prabhu, Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya Mahāprabhu and all their associates, and to the lotus feet of Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa, and to Their companions, Śrī Lalitā, Śrī Viśākhā, and all the other sakhīs.

content.sanskrit:
oṁ ajñāna-timirāndhasya jñānāñjana-śalākayā
cakṣur unmīlitaṁ yena tasmai śrī-gurave namaḥ
content.translation: I am blinded by the darkness of ignorance, but śrī guru has mercifully opened my eyes, anointing them with the salve of divine knowledge. I offer praṇāma to that śrī gurudeva.

content.sanskrit:
nārāyaṇa-parāyaṇa jadāhaṅkāra-nāśana
rūpānugāmṛta prema prayojanāya te namaḥ
content.translation: O you who are always absorbed in loving service to your spiritual master, Śrīla Bhaktivedānta Nārāyaṇa Gosvāmī! O you whose causeless mercy completely destroys the inert material ego! You are the embodiment of the immortal nectar of pure divine love (prema) in subordination to Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī, which is the highest goal of life (prayojana). I offer my praṇāma unto You, Śrī Prema Prayojana Prabhu.

subsections.title: Prayers to Śrī Śrīmad Bhaktivedānta Nārāyaṇa Gosvāmī Mahārāja

content.sanskrit:
nama oṁ viṣṇu-pādāya rādhikāyaḥ-priyātmane
śrī-śrīmad-bhaktivedānta-nārāyaṇa iti nāmine
content.translation: I offer praṇāma to oṁ viṣṇupāda Śrī Śrīmad Bhaktivedānta Nārāyaṇa Gosvāmī Mahārāja, who is very dear to Śrīmatī Rādhikā.

content.sanskrit:
śrī-kṛṣṇa-līlā-kathane su-dakṣam
audārya-mādhurya-guṇaiś ca yuktam
varaṁ vareṇyaṁ puruṣaṁ mahāntaṁ
nārāyaṇaṁ tvāṁ śirasā namāmi
content.translation: O Śrīla Bhaktivedānta Nārāyaṇa Gosvāmī Mahārāja, you are most expert in describing kṛṣṇa-līlā and are fully endowed with the qualities of magnanimity and sweetness. You are the best of all great souls. I bow my head to you.

content.sanskrit:
tridandīnām bhakta-śiromaṇiṁ ca
śrī-kṛṣṇa-padābja-dhṛtaika-hṛdi
caitanya-līlāmṛta-sāra-sāraṁ
nārāyāṇaṁ tvaṁ satataṁ prapadye
content.translation: Śrīla Nārāyaṇa Gosvāmī, the jewel among tridaṇḍī-sannyāsīs, holds the lotus feet of Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa within his heart and experiences supreme bliss when Kṛṣṇa renders service to Śrīmatī Rādhikā. He is always absorbed in thoughts of Śrī Caitanya Mahāprabhu and the confidential reasons for His divine descent. With deep reverence, I offer my prostrated obeisances at the lotus feet of Śrīla Nārāyaṇa Gosvāmī, who is endowed with innumerable transcendental qualities.

subsections.title: Prayers to Śrī Śrīmad Bhaktivedānta Svāmī Mahārāja

content.sanskrit:
nama oṁ viṣṇu-pādāya kṛṣṇa-preṣṭhāya bhūtale
śrīmate bhaktivedānta-svāmin iti nāmine
content.translation: I offer praṇāma to oṁ viṣṇupāda Śrī Śrīmad Bhaktivedānta Svāmī Mahārāja (Śrīla Prabhupāda), who in this world is most dear to Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

content.sanskrit:
namas te sārasvate deve gaura-vāṇī-pracāriṇe
nirviśeṣa-śūnyavādi-pāścātya-deśa-tāriṇe
content.translation: My respectful obeisances unto you, O servant of Sarasvatī Gosvāmī. You preach the message of Śrī Gaurāṅga and thus deliver the Western countries, which are filled with voidism and impersonalism.

subsections.title: Prayers to Śrī Śrīmad Bhakti Prajñāna Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja

content.sanskrit:
nama oṁ viṣṇu-pādāya ācārya-siṁha-rūpiṇe
śrī-śrīmad-bhakti-prajñāna-keśava iti nāmine
content.translation: I offer praṇāma to the lion-like ācārya, oṁ viṣṇupāda Śrī Śrīmad Bhakti Prajñāna Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja.

content.sanskrit:
atimartya-caritrāya svāśritānāṁ ca pāline
jīva-duḥkhe sadārttāya śrī-nāma-prema-dāyine
content.translation: His character and activities are transcendental to the modes of nature, and he protects and nurtures those who take shelter of him. He is always aggrieved to see the suffering of the living entities, and he bestows love for śrī nāma.

content.sanskrit:
gaurāśraya-vigrahāya kṛṣṇa-kāmaika-cāriṇe
rūpānuga-pravarāya vinodeti-svarūpiṇe
content.translation: He is the shelter of love for Gaurāṅga, and his every action is solely to fulfil the desires of Śrī Kṛṣṇa. He is best among the followers of Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī, and his internal identity is that of Vinoda Mañjarī.

content.sanskrit:
prabhupādāntaraṅgāya sarva-sad-guṇa-śāline
māyāvada-tamo-ghnāya vedāntārtha-vāde namaḥ
content.translation: He is an intimate associate of Śrīla Bhaktisiddhānta Sarasvatī Prabhupāda, and he is endowed with all auspicious qualities. He eradicates the darkness of māyāvada and reveals the true meaning of Vedānta. I offer praṇāma to him.

subsections.title: Prayers to Śrī Śrīmad Bhaktisiddhānta Sarasvatī Gosvāmī Prabhupāda

content.sanskrit:
nama oṁ viṣṇu-pādāya kṛṣṇa-preṣṭhāya bhūtale
śrīmate bhaktisiddhānta-sarasvatīti-nāmine
content.translation: I offer praṇāma to oṁ viṣṇupāda Śrī Śrīmad Bhaktisiddhānta Sarasvatī Gosvāmī Ṭhākura Prabhupāda, who, in this world, is most dear to Kṛṣṇa.

content.sanskrit:
śrī-vārṣabhānavī-devī-dayitāya kṛpābdhaye
kṛṣṇa-sambandha-vijñāna-dāyine prabhave namaḥ
content.translation: I offer praṇāma to Śrī Vārṣabhānavī-dayita dāsa, the manifestation of an ocean of mercy, who grants realization of our eternal relationship with Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

content.sanskrit:
mādhuryojjvala-premāḍhya-śrī-rūpānuga-bhakti-da
śrī-gaura-karuṇā-śakti-vigrahāya namo 'stu te
content.translation: O Śrīla Sarasvatī Ṭhākura, you deliver śrī rūpānuga-bhakti, which is enriched with ujjvala-mādhurya-prema. You are the embodiment of the mercy potency of Śrī Gaurāṅga. I offer praṇāma unto you.

content.sanskrit:
namas te gaura-vāṇī-śrī-mūrtaye dīna-tāriṇe
rūpānuga-viruddhāpasiddhānta-dhvānta-hāriṇe
content.translation: I offer obeisances to you, the embodiment of Śrī Gaurāṅga's message. You deliver the fallen and remove the darkness of philosophical misconceptions that are opposed to the precepts taught by Śrīla Rūpa Gosvāmī.

subsections.title: Prayer to Śrīla Gaura-kiśora dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja

content.sanskrit:
namo gaura-kiśorāya sākṣād-vairāgya-mūrtaye
vipralambha-rasāmbhodhe! pādāmbujāya te namaḥ
content.translation: I offer praṇāma to Śrī Gaura-kiśora dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja, who is the direct embodiment of renunciation. O you who are an ocean of vipralambha-rasa, I offer praṇāma unto your lotus feet.

content.sanskrit:
namo bhaktivinodāya sac-cid-ānanda-nāmine
gaura-śakti-svarūpāya rūpānuga-varāya te
content.translation: I offer praṇāma to you, Śrīla Saccidānanda Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura, the foremost of rūpānugas and the personal manifestation of Śrī Gaurāṅga's śakti [Gadādhara Paṇḍita].

subsections.title: Prayer to Śrīla Jagannātha dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja

content.sanskrit:
gaurāvirbhāva-bhūmes tvaṁ nirdeṣṭā sajjana-priyaḥ
vaiṣṇava-sārvabhauma śrī-jagannāthāya te namaḥ
content.translation: You indicated the place of Śrī Gaurāṅga's appearance, and you are most dear to the saintly. O Śrīla Jagannātha dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja, leader of the Vaiṣṇavas, I offer praṇāma unto you.

content.sanskrit:
vāñchā-kalpa-tarubhyaś ca kṛpā-sindhubhya eva ca
patitānāṁ pāvanebhyo vaiṣṇavebhyo namo namaḥ
content.translation: I offer praṇāma again and again to the Vaiṣṇavas, who are the saviors of the fallen, who are just like wish-fulfilling desire-trees and who are oceans of mercy.

content.sanskrit:
namo mahā-vadānyāya kṛṣṇa-prema-pradāya te
kṛṣṇāya kṛṣṇa-caitanya-nāmne gaura-tviṣe namaḥ
content.translation: I offer praṇāma to that greatly munificent Lord who bestows kṛṣṇa-prema. He is Kṛṣṇa Himself, who has assumed a golden complexion and accepted the name Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya.

content.sanskrit:
he kṛṣṇa karuṇā-sindho dīna-bandho jagat-pate
gopeśa gopikā-kānta rādhā-kānta namo 'stu te
content.translation: O Śrī Kṛṣṇa, You are an ocean of mercy, the friend of the fallen, Lord of the universe, master of the cowherds, beloved of the gopīs, and [above all] the beloved of Śrī Rādhā. I offer praṇāma unto You.

content.sanskrit:
tapta-kāñcana-gaurāṅgi rādhe vṛndāvaneśvari
vṛṣabhānu-sute devi praṇamāmi hari-priye
content.translation: O Śrī Rādhā, O Gaurāṅgi, whose complexion is like molten gold, You are the queen of Vṛndāvana, the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu, and the divine goddess, who is most dear to Śrī Hari. I offer praṇāma unto You.

subsections.title: Prayer to the Presiding Deity of Sambandha, Śrī Rādhā-Madana-mohana

content.sanskrit:
jayatāṁ su-ratau paṅgor mama manda-mater gatī
mat-sarvasva padāmbhojau rādhā-madana-mohanau
content.translation: All glories to the supremely merciful Śrī Rādhā-Madana-mohana! Although I am lame and foolish, They are my sole refuge. Their lotus feet are everything to me.

subsections.title: Prayer to the Presiding Deity of Abhidheya, Śrī Rādhā-Govinda

content.sanskrit:
dīvyad-vṛndāraṇya-kalpa-drumādhaḥ
śrīmad-ratnāgāra-siṁhāsana-sthau
śrīmad-rādhā-śrīla-govinda-devau
preṣṭhālībhiḥ sevyamānau smarāmi
content.translation: In the shining land of Śrī Vṛndāvana, in a temple composed of jewels, Śrī Rādhā-Govinda are seated on an effulgent throne beneath a kalpa-vṛkṣa tree, accepting service from Their beloved sakhīs. I meditate upon Them.

subsections.title: Prayer to the Presiding Deity of Prayojana, Śrī Rādhā-Gopīnātha

content.sanskrit:
śrīmān rāsa-rasārambhī vaṁśīvaṭa-taṭa-sthitaḥ
karṣan veṇu-svanair gopīr gopīnāthaḥ śriye 'stu naḥ
content.translation: Standing on the river-bank at Vaṁśīvaṭa, Śrī Gopīnātha, the initiator of the transcendental mellow of the rāsa-dance, attracts all the gopīs with the sound of His veṇu flute. May He confer auspiciousness upon us.

content.sanskrit:
vṛndāyai tulasī-devyai priyāyai keśavasya ca
kṛṣṇa-bhakti-prade devi! satyavatyai namo namaḥ
content.translation: I offer praṇāma again and again to Śrīmatī Tulasī-devī, who is most dear to Śrī Keśava and who is also renowned as Vṛndā-devī and Satyavatī. O Devī, you bestow kṛṣṇa-bhakti.

content.sanskrit:
pañca-tattvātmakaṁ kṛṣṇaṁ bhakta-rūpa-svarūpakam
bhaktāvatāraṁ bhaktākhyaṁ namāmi bhakta-śaktikam
content.translation: I offer praṇāma unto Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya Mahāprabhu in His five features as bhakta-rūpa (Mahāprabhu), bhakta-svarūpa (Nityānanda Prabhu), bhakta-avatāra (Advaita Ācārya), bhakta (Śrīvāsa Ṭhākura) and bhakta-śakti (Gadādhara Paṇḍita).

content.sanskrit:
śrī-kṛṣṇa-caitanya prabhu-nityānanda
śrī-advaita gadādhara śrīvāsādi-gaura-bhakta-vṛnda

hare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare hare
hare rāma hare rāma rāma rāma hare hare

sections.title: Daily Duties performed in the Brāhma-muhūrta

content.content: Right after awaking chant pranama mantra to Your Gurudeva.
Śrī Śrī Guru-Gaurāṅga Gāndhārvikā-Giridhārī Rādhā-Vinoda-Bihārījī kī jaya
And chanting the pañca-tattva-mantra and the mahā-mantra. With heartfelt devotion, chant the following prayers:

content.sanskrit:
jayati jana-nivāso devakī-janma-vādo
yadu-vara-pariṣat svair dorbhir asyann adharmam
sthira-cara-vṛjina-ghnaḥ su-smita-śrī-mukhena
vraja-pura-vanitānāṁ vardhayan kāmadevam
content.translation: All glories to that Yaśodā-nandana -- around whom a controversy arose because He seemingly took birth from the womb of Devakī, although later, that became uncertain -- who resides within the heart of all. His assembly is decorated with the presence of the exalted Yādavas, and He destroys irreligion with His own might and the help of His devotees. The chanting of His name removes inauspiciousness from all living entities, both moving and stationary. All glories to Vrajendra-nandana Śrī Kṛṣṇa, who's softly smiling lotus face always enhances the transcendental lust of the vraja-gopīs. [Śrī-mad-Bhāgavatam 10.90.48]

content.sanskrit:
vidagdha-gopāla-vilāsinīnāṁ
sambhoga-cihnāṅkita-sarva-gātram
pavitram āmnāya girām agamyaṁ
brahma prapadye navanīta-cauram
content.translation: I take shelter of He whose relish of rasa is topmost, the Supreme Absolute Truth, Gopāla Śrī Kṛṣṇa, who bears on different parts of His body signs of amorous union with the expert gopīs, who steals butter, and who is incomprehensible through Vedic statements.

content.sanskrit:
udgāyatīnām aravinda-locananaṁ
vrajāṅganānāṁ divam aspṛśad dhvaniḥ
dadhnaś ca nirmanthana-śabda-miśrito
nirasyate yena diśām amaṅgalam
content.translation: As it mixes with the sound of churning yoghurt, the vraja-gopīs' loud singing of sweet and melodious songs that glorify the name, form, qualities and pastimes of lotus-eyed Śrī Kṛṣṇa pervades the entire atmosphere and removes inauspiciousness in all directions.

content.content: Then one should brush one's teeth, wash face and then bathe after passing stool and put on clean clothes. Pujari do not go to the toilet in clean clothes!

Long nails are dirty and contaminated. Therefore, it is a general practice of pūjārīs to keep their fingernails short, to avoid contaminating the bhoga and other articles meant for offering to the Lord. Even during the four months of Cāturmāsya, pūjārīs keep their nails short. Brush their teeth even on fast days.
Mantra for taking bath by mind (mānasa-snāna)

content.sanskrit:
oṁ apavitraḥ pavitro vā
sarvāvasthāṁ gato 'pi vā
yaḥ smaret puṇḍarīkākṣaṁ
sa bāhyābhyantara-śuciḥ
content.translation: Whether one is contaminated, pure or in any condition or stage of life, if one remembers the lotus-eyed Lord, Puṇḍarīkākṣa, Śrī Kṛṣṇa, one becomes pure both externally and internally. [Garuḍa Purāṇa]

content.content: After bathing, comb the hair and tie the śikhā while remembering the brahma-gāyatrī, along with the syllable oṁ.
The śikhā is not braided or kept untied and disheveled. It is preferable to tie it with a slip knot.

Do not enter the deity room or the kitchen with contaminated cloth or touch the deity paraphernalia and kitchen pots. Do not clean the deity's paraphernalia or wash the deity's pots while wearing cloth in which one has defecated or slept the night before.
Tīrtha-āvāhana-mantra

Set an empty pañca-pātra in place and fill it with fresh water. After pouring a few drops of water from the ācamana-pātra or a spouted waterpot onto the fingers of your right hand, purify the pañca-pātra by prokṣaṇa:
Chanting oṁ astrāya phaṭ, sprinkle water on the pañca-pātra.
Invoke the Gaṅgā and other holy rivers by showing the aṅkuśamudrā and chanting:

content.sanskrit:
gaṅge ca yamune caiva
godāvari sarasvati
narmade sindho kāveri
jale 'smin sannidhiṁ kuru
content.translation: O Ganges, O Yamunā, O Godāvarī, O Sarasvatī, O Narmadā, O Sindhu, O Kāverī, please become present in this water.

content.content: After sitting on a clean āsana (seat), pour Ganges water into the pañca-pātra. In the absence of Ganges water, put fresh water in the pañca-pātra. Then, remembering the Ganges and other sacred rivers, touch this water, and chant the tīrtha-āvāhana-mantra.

Use this water and gopī-candana to form a paste in the left hand. Then, with the twelve mantras beginning with oṁ keśavāya namaḥ, apply tilaka to the twelve different parts of the body, beginning with the forehead, by drawing the ūrdhva-puṇḍra (two vertical lines) representing the temple of Śrī Hari.

Starting from the centre of the eyebrows, form two vertical lines on the forehead that reach up to the beginning of the hairline. There must be a clean space in the middle of the ūrdhva-puṇḍra. From just below the centre of the eyebrows extending to three-quarters of the way down the nose is called the nāsāmūla. [This is where one forms the shape of a tulasī leaf.] The mantra to invoke remembrance of the twelve names of the Lord (dvādaśa-nāma-smaraṇa-mantra) is as follows:

content.sanskrit:
lalāṭe keśavaṁ dhyāyen
nārāyaṇam athodare
vakṣaḥ-sthale mādhavaṁ tu
govindaṁ kaṇṭha-kūpake

viṣṇuṁ ca dakṣiṇe kukṣau
bāhau ca madhusūdanam
trivikramaṁ kandhare tu
vāmanaṁ vāma-pārśvake

śrīdharaṁ vāma-bāhau tu
hṛṣīkeśaṁ ca kandhare
pṛṣṭhe tu padmanābhaṁ ca
kaṭyāṁ dāmodaraṁ nyaset

tat prakṣālana-toyaṁ tu
vāsudevāya mūrdhani
content.translation: When one marks the forehead with tilaka, one must remember Keśava. When one marks the lower abdomen, one must remember Nārāyaṇa. When marking the chest, one should remember Mādhava, and when marking the hollow of the neck one should remember Govinda. Viṣṇu should be remembered while marking the right side of the belly, and Madhusūdana should be remembered when marking the right arm. Trivikrama should be remembered when marking the right shoulder, and Vāmana should be remembered when marking the left side of the belly. Śrīdhara should be remembered while marking the left arm, and Hṛṣīkeśa should be remembered when marking the left shoulder. Padmanābha and Dāmodara should be remembered when marking the upper and lower back, and Vāsudeva should be remembered when marking the top of the head.

content.content: In accordance with the aforementioned smaraṇa-mantra, chant the following mantras while touching each part of the body, either while applying the tilaka mark or after the tilaka has already been applied:
Drop a spoonful of water from the pañca-pātra into the hand, to mix with the remnant gopī-candana. Wipe the candana on top of the head, while chanting oṁ vāsudevāya namaḥ.
Note: Tilaka paste is to be freshly made each time. Do not apply Tilaka in the bathroom.
A pujari who chants twelve names of Vishnu always remembers only Rādhārani.

subsections.title: Performing Ācamana, sipping water for purification

content.content: After applying tilaka, one should perform ācamana. Vaiṣṇava ācamana is of two kinds: ordinary (sādhāraṇa) and specific (viśeṣa). Before performing any devotional activity, ordinary ācamana will suffice. However, at the time of bathing the deity or at the time of pūjā, viśeṣa-ācamana is to be performed.
Sādhāraṇa-ācamana
Drop a spoonful of water [from the pañca-pātra, or ācamana cup] into the centre of the palm of the right hand (known as the brāhma-tīrtha) and chant

content.sanskrit: oṁ keśavāya namaḥ

content.content: Sip a little of the water [from the end of the palm] and throw the rest to the ground.
Purify the right hand [by dropping a new spoonful of water in the palm of the right hand and then throwing that water to the ground].
Repeat the above process of ācamana, this time while chanting

content.sanskrit: oṁ nārāyaṇāya namaḥ

content.content: and then again while chanting

content.sanskrit: oṁ mādhavāya namaḥ

content.content: Viśeṣa-ācamana
Always perform ācamana before entering the Deity room to worship the Lord.

With an ācamana-pātra filled with water clean both hands by sprinkling them with water; holding the ācamana spoon in your left hand, pour a few drops into the right palm. While looking at the water chant the appropriate mantra and then silently sip the water from the base of the right thumb (the brāhma-tīrtha). The procedure for basic ācamana consists of the first three steps below. If time and circumstances permit, you may perform the complete ācamana:

items.label: oṁ keśavāya namaḥ
items.value: sip water from the brāhma-tīrtha of your right hand.

items.label: oṁ nārāyaṇāya namaḥ
items.value: sip water from the brāhma-tīrtha of your right hand.

items.label: oṁ mādhavāya namaḥ
items.value: sip water from the brāhma-tīrtha of your right hand.

items.label: oṁ govindāya namaḥ
items.value: sprinkle water on your right hand.

items.label: oṁ viṣṇave namaḥ
items.value: sprinkle water on your left hand.

items.label: oṁ madhusūdanāya namaḥ
items.value: touch your right cheek with the fingers of your right hand.

items.label: oṁ trivikramāya namaḥ
items.value: touch your left cheek with the fingers of your right hand.

items.label: oṁ vāmanāya namaḥ
items.value: wipe above your upper lip with the base of your right thumb.

items.label: oṁ śrīdharāya namaḥ
items.value: wipe below your lower lip with the base of your right thumb.

items.label: oṁ dāmodarāya namaḥ
items.value: sprinkle water on the top of your head.

content.content: While chanting the following mantras, perform the hand motions:

items.label: oṁ vāsudevāya namaḥ
items.value: touch your upper and lower lips with the tips of the fingers of your right hand.

items.label: oṁ saṅkarṣaṇāya namaḥ
items.value: touch your right nostril with your right thumb and forefinger.

items.label: oṁ pradyumnāya namaḥ
items.value: touch your left nostril with your right thumb and forefinger.

items.label: oṁ aniruddhāya namaḥ
items.value: touch your right eye with your right thumb and ring finger.

items.label: oṁ puruṣottamāya namaḥ
items.value: touch your left eye with your right thumb and ring finger.

items.label: oṁ adhokṣajāya namaḥ
items.value: touch your right ear with your right thumb and forefinger.

items.label: oṁ nṛsiṁhāya namaḥ
items.value: touch your left ear with your right thumb and forefinger.

items.label: oṁ acyutāya namaḥ
items.value: touch your navel with your right thumb and little finger.

items.label: oṁ janārdanāya namaḥ
items.value: touch your heart with the palm of your right hand.

items.label: oṁ upendrāya namaḥ
items.value: touch your head with all the finger tips of your right hand.

items.label: oṁ haraye namaḥ
items.value: touch your right upper arm with the fingertips of your left hand.

items.label: oṁ kṛṣṇāya namaḥ
items.value: touch your left upper arm with the fingertips of your right hand.

content.content: Now chant the following mantra from the Ṛig Veda while showing the praṇāma-mudrā (palms together in front of your heart):

content.sanskrit:
oṁ tad viṣṇoḥ paramaṁ padaṁ
sadā paśyanti sūrayaḥ
divīva cakṣur-ātatam

tad viprāso vipanyavo
jāgṛvaṁ saḥ samindhate
viṣṇor yat paramaṁ padam
content.translation: Just as those with ordinary vision see the sun's rays in the sky, so the wise and learned devotees always see the supreme abode of Lord Vishnu. Because those highly praise-worthy and spiritually awake brāhmaṇas can see that abode, they can also reveal it to others. [Rig Veda Samhita]

sections.title: Daily Deity Schedule
sections.subtitle: Procedure for Waking up the Deities

content.content: The pūjārī should arrive no later than 3:20 a.m.
Perform Viśeṣa-ācamana (full ācamana)
Make up 1 tray with: Sweets, sweet rice, cup water, cup milk. Note: place tulasī on each preparation.
Make up āratī tray with: Incense, ghee lamp, flowers, handkerchief, water, cotton stick for perfume oil.
Prayers to be chanted upon entering the Deity room
Remaining outside the Deity room, ring the bell.
Reciting following verses, pray to the Lord to awaken.

content.sanskrit:
so 'sāv adabhra-karuṇo bhagavān vivṛddha-
prema-smitena nayanāmburuhaṁ vijṛmbhan
utthāya viśva-vijayāya ca no viṣādaṁ
mādhvyā girāpanayatāt puruṣaḥ purāṇaḥ
content.translation: Śrī Bhagavān, who is supreme and primeval, is unlimitedly merciful. I pray that He bless me with His smiling glance by opening His lotus eyes. He can uplift the entire cosmic creation and remove our grief and sorrow with the sweet words of instruction emanating from His lotus mouth. [SB 3.9.25]

content.sanskrit:
deva prapannārti-hara prasādaṁ kuru keśava
avalokana-dānena bhūyo māṁ parayācyuta
content.translation: Keśava, O infallible one, O remover of the distress of the surrendered souls, kindly bestow Your mercy upon me by again awarding me with Your transcendental glance.

content.sanskrit:
jaya jaya kṛpamaya jagatera nātha
sarva jagatere koro śubha-dṛṣṭī-pāta
content.translation: O Bhagavān! All glories, all glories unto You, O merciful Lord of the universe, please cast Your auspicious glance upon all the worlds.

content.content: Then knock on the door and enter the Deity room.
Turn on a soft light and sit down on the āsana.
Perform Sādhāraṇa-ācamana (ordinary ācamana) and, while ringing the bell, proceed to the place where Srī Gurudeva is resting. Awaken him by touching his feet and chant the following prayers.
Prayers in Deity's room
Touching the feet of Śrī Gurudev, awaken Him by chanting:

content.sanskrit:
uttiṣṭhottiṣṭha śrī guro
tyaja nidrāṁ kṛpāmaya
aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: O most merciful Gurudeva, please arise, please arise and give up your sleep.

content.content: Then, touching the feet of Śrī Gaurāṅga, awaken Him by chanting:

content.sanskrit:
uttiṣṭhottiṣṭha gaurāṅga
jahi nidrāṁ mahāprabho
śubha-dṛṣṭi pradānena
trailokya-maṅgalaṁ kuru
klīng gaurāya svāhā
content.translation: O Gaurāṅga Mahāprabhu, please arise, please arise and give up Your sleep. Please benedict the three worlds with Your auspicious glance.

content.content: Next, while touching the lotus feet of Śrī Rādhā Krsna awaken Them by chanting:

content.sanskrit:
go-gopa-gokulānanda
yaśodānanda vardhana
uttiṣṭha rādhayā sārddhaṁ
prātar āsīj jagat-pate
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ
content.translation: O joy of Gokula, the cowherd boys and the cows; O increaser of Mother Yaśodā's bliss; O Lord of the universe; please arise along with Śrī Rādhā, for it is almost morning.

content.content: Afterward, meditate that these personalities have risen and taken their seat on the altar.
(Physically place the Deities on the altar if They were removed from there the previous night and placed in a bed.)
Carefully replace the Chota Deities on the altar, put crown on Deities.
Clean and cover the bed.

subsections.title: Washing the mouth of the Lord

content.content: Now, while chanting the following mantras, one should first offer ācamana (water for cleansing the mouth) to Sri Guru and the deity. Then offer a twig for cleaning the teeth (danta-kāṣṭha), and again ācamana:

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ ācamanīyaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
idaṁ ācamanīyaṁ klīng gaurāya svāhā
idaṁ ācamanīyaṁ śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ

content.content: Note: Ācamana is offered by tipping a spoonful of water from the pañca-pātra into an empty container (visarjanīya-pātra), while ringing the bell and saying the respective mantra.

content.sanskrit:
eṣa danta-kāṣṭhaḥ aing gurave namaḥ
eṣa danta-kāṣṭhaḥ klīng gaurāya svāhā
eṣa danta-kāṣṭhaḥ śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ

content.content: The above mantras signify the offering of a twig for cleaning the teeth (danta-kāṣṭha). This item is offered by mind rather than by offering the actual article. Generally, in the absence of any item, one may meditate on the particular item and, while saying the appropriate mantra, offer water from the pañca-pātra into the throw-out pot.
Now offer ācamana like before, but 12 times. (3 times is also acceptable.)

subsections.title: Cleaning the Deities and the altar

content.content: Wipe the face, hands and feet of each Deity with a clean cloth. After removing old garlands, tulasī leaves and flowers, clean the altar and then purify one's hands. Offer tulasī and decorate each Deity with the appropriate crown, flute and ornaments while chanting Their appropriate mūla-mantra. Then perform maṅgala-ārati.
Replace crowns and Kṛṣṇa's staff and water cups. Make bed. Place out sitting mats for bhoga offering and clean offering tables.
4:10 a.m. Offer bhoga (follow the bhoga offering procedure).
4:25 a.m. Perform ācamanīyaṁ. Remove plates. Clean tables. Bring in āratī tray. Light candle. Replace Kṛṣṇa's flute.
4:30 a.m. Blow conch, open curtain, offer ārati.
4:50 a.m. Finish ārati, blow conch. Offer devotees prasād flowers. Clean and put away ārati tray.
4:55 a.m. Offer scent. Clean floor.
Dressing Their Lordships (in progress)
7:10 a.m. The small Deities and Tulasī are placed on the altar. Clean floor. Light and offer incense. Offer mirror.
7:15 a.m. Blow conch, open curtain. Offer cāmara and peacock fan (not in winter).
7:50 a.m. Make up plate of: Daily sweet 2 piece; halava; kitchari; subji; puris 2 each; milk; water; fruit salad.
8:00 a.m. Close Deity curtain, use small bell during class. Clean tables and floor, set out sitting mats, bring in offering. Remove Kṛṣṇa's flute.
8:10 a.m. Breakfast Offering (follow the bhoga offering procedure).
8:25 a.m. Offer Deities ācamanīyaṁ. Remove plates, pick up asana, replace Kṛṣṇa's flute, bring in incense and flower plate for ārati.
8:30 a.m. Dhūpa-ārati. Open Deity curtain. Offer incense; flowers; cāmara and peacock fan (not in winter) 10 mins. Clean altar.
11:40 a.m. Pūjārī makes bhoga plates.
11:50 a.m. Close Deity curtain, wipe tables with wet cloth. Clean floor. Remove flute, place asanas, put flower petals on them. Bring in offering plates.
11:55 a.m. Make offering bhoga (see offering procedure). Make up full āratī trays: incense; camphor lamp, ghee lamp; water; handkerchief; flowers; cāmara and peacock fan (not in winter).
12:25 p.m. Enter altar knocking the door, remove plates, replace flute.
12:30 noon. Blow conch and open curtains. Offer full ārati.
12:55 p.m. Finish ārati.
Sweep and wash the floor.
Close curtains.
Uncover beds.
After this, offer fragrant drinks, tāmbūla with camphor, a garland and some flowers. [Or one may simply place a fresh glass of water before the Deities.]
Chant prayers for putting Deities to rest while touching Their lotus feet.

subsections.title: Procedure for Putting the Deities to Rest (Noon)

content.content: After the noontime ārati, remove the crowns, flute from Deities, and pray to Them to lay down on Their bed to take rest (śayana) by saying:
Rādhā-Krsna

content.sanskrit:
āgaccha śayana-sthānaṁ
priyābhiḥ saha keśava
divya-puṣpātya-śayyāyāṁ
sukhaṁ vihara mādhava
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ
content.translation: O Keśava, kindly come to Your bed along with Your beloved Śrīmatī Rādhikā. O Mādhava, this effulgent bed of fragrant flowers is made for Your joyful sporting.

content.content: Lord Caitanya Mahāprabhu

content.sanskrit:
āgaccha viśrama-sthānaṁ
sva-gaṇaiḥ saha gaurāṅga
kṣaṇaṁ viśramya sukhena
līlayā vihara prabhu
klīng gaurāya svāhā
content.translation: O Lord Gaurāṅga, please come to Your resting place along with Your associates. O Lord, rest comfortably for a moment, enjoying Your pastimes.

content.content: Guru
Then invite Srī Gurudeva to his resting place with the following mantra:

content.sanskrit:
āgaccha viśrāma-sthānaṁ
sva-gaṇaiḥ saha śrī-guro
aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: O Gurudeva, kindly come to your resting place along with all your associates.

content.content: 3:15 p.m. Cut and prepare fruit bhoga offering. Make up offering plate: 2 cookies; 3 bowls fruit; 1 drink; water (1 bowl honey for Balarāma).
3:35 p.m. Wake up the Deities (see morning procedure).
3:40 p.m. Make offering (see offering procedure).
3:55 p.m. Remove plate. Perform ācamana for Deities. Remove asanas, clean tables, floor, etc.
4:00 p.m. Dhūpa-ārati: Open curtains, offer incense; flowers; cāmara; peacock fan (not in winter).
4:10 p.m. Finish ārati, clean up, wash floor, etc.
6:10 p.m. Make offering plate: 3 subjis; 4 puris; 1 halava; 1 milk; water; 2 daily sweets.
6:30 p.m. Close curtains. Set out asanas etc. for offering.
6:40 p.m. Offer bhoga and make up full āratī trays.
6:55 p.m. Remove plates. Perform ācamana for Deities. Remove asanas, clean tables, floor, etc.
7:00 p.m. Sandhya ārati.
7:25 p.m. Finish ārati.
7:45 p.m. Make offering plate: 1 subji; 2 puris; 1 halava; 1 milk; water; 2 daily sweets.
8:00 p.m. Close curtains and prepare for offering.
8:10 p.m. Offer bhoga.
8:25 p.m. Remove plate. Perform ācamana for Deities. Remove asanas, clean tables, floor, etc.
8:30 p.m. Dhūpa-ārati: incense; flowers; cāmara; peacock fan (not in winter).
8:40 p.m. Finish ārati and distribute garlands.
8:45 p.m. Close curtains.
Remove Tulasī devi from altar.
Remove flowers and garlands.
Undress Deities.
Dress simply with little jewellery.
Small Deities putting to bed.
Bring in and offer kheer and water.
Put Deities to rest with prayers and mantras.

subsections.title: Procedure for Putting the Deities to Rest (Evening)

content.content: After the evening ārati, remove the crowns, flute from Deities, and pray to Them to lay down on Their bed to take rest (śayana) by saying:
Rādhā-Krsna

content.sanskrit:
āgaccha śayana-sthānaṁ
priyābhiḥ saha keśava
divya-puṣpātya-śayyāyāṁ
sukhaṁ vihara mādhava
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ
content.translation: O Keśava, kindly come to Your bed along with Your beloved Śrīmatī Rādhikā. O Mādhava, this effulgent bed of fragrant flowers is made for Your joyful sporting.

content.content: Lord Caitanya Mahāprabhu

content.sanskrit:
āgaccha viśrama-sthānaṁ
sva-gaṇaiḥ saha gaurāṅga
kṣaṇaṁ viśramya sukhena
līlayā vihara prabhu
klīng gaurāya svāhā
content.translation: O Lord Gaurāṅga, please come to Your resting place along with Your associates. O Lord, rest comfortably for a moment, enjoying Your pastimes.

content.content: Guru
Then invite Srī Gurudeva to his resting place with the following mantra:

content.sanskrit:
āgaccha viśrāma-sthānaṁ
sva-gaṇaiḥ saha śrī-guro
aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: O Gurudeva, kindly come to your resting place along with all your associates.

content.content: Chant mantras for forgiveness (Prayers Begging Forgiveness for Offences).

sections.title: Worship of Lord Caitanya and Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa with sixteen articles
sections.subtitle: Ṣoḍaśopacāra-pūjā

content.content: Outside the Deity room (Pujari room) perform ācamana and offer obeisances to your spiritual master.
Pujari collects the necessary paraphernalia for the worship of Deities.
To avoid interrupting the worship, see to it that the following items are present before beginning:
Enter in the Deity room, clean the area where you will do the worship and place an asana on the floor.
Carry all required paraphernalia into the Deity room.
Place the asana in position for performing the worship and, while kneeling, with joined palms (pranama-mudra) chant:

content.sanskrit:
pṛthvi tvayā dhṛtā lokā
devi tvaṃ viṣṇunā dhṛtā
tvaṃ ca dhāraya māṃ nityaṃ
pavitraṃ kuru cāsanam
content.translation: O Mother Earth, O goddess, you support all the world's people, and Lord Visnu sustains you; please be merciful today and support me as I sit to worship the Lord.

content.content: While thinking of your spiritual master sitting on an asana in position for performing the worship, sit on your own asana.
Arrange the items in such a way that you can easily reach them without touching offered to un-offered items.

Place the bell (on a plate) to your left and place the bathing conch on a stand so you can easily lift and replace it several times. The waterpot, flowers, candana, and tulasī leaves should be within easy reach.

Use the throw-out pot (visarjanīya-pātra) for offered ācamana and arghya, or you may pour these liquids into the bathing receptacle (snāna-pātra) if it is a receptacle which has a drain. You may use a second visarjanīya-pātra for offered flower petals, cotton swabs, etc.
Bathing towels should be placed on a tray, table or the altar, not on your lap or directly on the floor. This applies to all paraphernalia; nothing should be placed on the lower cloth or the floor.

subsections.title: Requesting the Spiritual Master's and Previous Ācāryas' Blessings (guru-paṅkti-namaskāra)

content.content: Preliminary Worship
Begin the arcana of Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa by seeking the permission and mercy of Sri Guru and Śrī Gaurāṅga:
Chant: Tvaṁ gopikā vṛṣa-raves tanayāntike ‘si..., Oṁ ajñāna-timirāndhasya..., Nāma-śreṣṭhaṁ manum api śacī-putram atra svarūpaṁ..., Vāñchā-kalpa-tarubhyaś ca..., Maha mantra

Śrī Gurudeva is a confidential servant and an intimate friend (sakhī) of Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa. Gurudeva is engaged in the direct service of Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa. Meditating like this and remembering one's incapability, perform arcana.
Draw the attention of your spiritual master by placing flowers before him while chanting

content.sanskrit: eṣa puṣpāñjaliḥ aing gurave namaḥ

content.content: Then, meditating on your spiritual master's service to the previous ācāryas, show the praṇāma-mudrā (joined palms) and pray for his blessings to assist in the worship of the Lord.
Offer praṇāma to the guru-paramparā, situated on the altar, then recite:

content.sanskrit: aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: I offer obeisances to my spiritual master.

content.sanskrit: aing parama-gurave namaḥ
content.translation: I offer obeisances to my grand spiritual master.

content.sanskrit: aing parameṣṭhi gurave namaḥ
content.translation: I offer obeisances to my great grand spiritual master.

content.sanskrit: aing śrī guru-paramparāyai namaḥ
content.translation: I offer obeisances to śrī guru-paramparā.

content.sanskrit: oṁ sarva-gurūttamāya śrī kṛṣṇa-caitanyāya namaḥ
content.translation: I offer obeisances to the topmost of all gurus, Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya.

content.content: Wash your hands with water from the waterpot.
Sprinkle the flowers with Samānya-arghya (holy rivers) water while chanting:

content.sanskrit: oṁ astrāya phaṭ

oṁ puṣpe puṣpe mahā-puṣpe
su-puṣpe puṣpa-sambhave
puṣpe cayavākīrṇe ca
huṁ phaṭ svāhā
content.translation: O flowers, O great and auspicious flowers who have appeared from a budding creeper, may you be purified.

content.content: Sprinkle all the paraphernalia lightly with samānya-arghya water while chanting:

content.sanskrit: oṁ astrāya phaṭ

content.content: Purifying the bodily elements by identifying oneself as the eternal servant of the servant of Lord Kṛṣṇa

Before arcana, one purifies oneself by thinking: "By constitution I am an eternal servant of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, but by some misfortune, I became averse to Him since time immemorial. Having thus identified myself as a material body in this illusory existence and having been trapped in this repeated cycle of birth and death, I was burning in the blazing fire of the threefold miseries. However, by great fortune -- that is, by the mercy of Sri Guru -- I have come to understand my identity as a minute, atomic spiritual particle, an eternal servant of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, completely aloof from the gross and subtle bodies. Now, by the order of Sri Gurudeva, under his shelter, I have obtained the fortune of serving Śrī Guru-Gaurāṅga and Śrī Rādhā-Vinoda-Bihārījī."

content.sanskrit:
nāhaṁ vipro na ca nara-patir nāpi vaiśyo na śūdro
nāhaṁ varṇī na ca gṛha-patir no vana-stho yatir vā
kintu prodyan-nikhila-paramānanda-pūrṇāmṛtābdher
gopī-bhartuḥ pada-kamālāyor dāsa-dāsānudāsaḥ
content.translation: I am not a brāhmaṇa, kṣatriya, vaiśya or śūdra, nor am I a brahmacārī, gṛhastha, vānaprastha or sannyāsī. Being transcendental to this gross and subtle body, I am the servant of the servant of the servant of the lotus feet of the master of the gopīs, Śrī Kṛṣṇa, who is the ultimate shelter of everyone, full of transcendental bliss and an ocean of unlimited nectar. [Padyāvalī 74]

content.sanskrit:
divyaṁ śrī-hari-mandirāḍhya-tilakaṁ kaṇṭhaṁ sumālānvitaṁ
vakṣaḥ śrī-harināma-varṇa-subhagaṁ śrī-khaṇḍa-liptaṁ punaḥ
pūtaṁ sūkṣmaṁ navāmbaraṁ vimalatāṁ nityaṁ vahantīṁ tanuṁ
dhyāyet śrī-guru-pāda-padma-nikaṭe sevotsukaṁ cātmanaḥ
content.translation: I meditate on myself as being surrendered at the lotus feet of śrī guru and possessing great determination to serve him. I meditate on myself, with my forehead adorned with beautiful tilaka, representing the temple of Śrī Hari. I am wearing a beautiful necklace of tulasī beads. My chest is marked with the holy name and anointed with sandalwood pulp. I am dressed in fine and pure cloth, and within, I am constantly absorbed in ever-fresh spiritual sentiments.

content.content: Note: In brief, before one begins arcana, one should clean the altar and the paraphernalia. Place the articles in a convenient place. It is important to sanctify the water in the pañca-pātra with the mantra "gaṅge ca yamune caiva..." and to purify flowers with the mantra "puṣpe puṣpe mahā-puṣpe...". Pādya, arghya and ācamanīya are usually substituted with pure water from the pañca-pātra. Similarly, if any item cannot be procured, it can be substituted by meditation and the offering of water from the pañca-pātra or conch shell.
While offering the bell a flower petal dipped in candana, chant:

content.sanskrit: ete gandha-puspe oṁ jaya-dhuani-mantra-matāh svāhā

content.content: Now affix the flower petal to the body of the bell with the candana. Then ring the bell briefly with your left hand and replace it on the plate, all the while thinking of the bell as a servant of the Lord.
While offering flowers and candana to the Lord's bathing conch, chant:

content.sanskrit:
ete gandha-puspe
oṁ hum hum hum namah maha-sankhaya svāhā

content.content: See "Arcana of Sri Guru" section.
Then chant the prema-dhvani for your spiritual master: jaya oṁ viṣṇupāda [name of your spiritual master] kī jaya.

subsections.title: Worshipping Lord Caitanya and Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa with sixteen upacāras (bāhya-pūjā)

content.content: Meditation on Śrī Vṛndāvana-dhāma

content.sanskrit:
tato vṛndāvanaṁ dhyāyet paramānanda-vardhanam
kālindī-jala-kallola- saṅgi-māruta-sevitam
nānā-puṣpa-latā-baddha- vṛkṣa-ṣaṇḍaiś ca maṇḍitam
koṭi-sūrya-samābhāsaṁ vimuktaṁ ṣaṭ-taraṅgakaiḥ
tan-madhye ratna-khacitaṁ svarṇa-siṁhāsanaṁ mahat
content.translation: Meditate on a great golden throne studded with jewels within the divine realm of Śrī Vṛndāvana-dhāma. A place of ever-increasing bliss, Vṛndāvana-dhāma is served by breezes cooled by the touch of the Yamunā's waves and is decorated with various types of flowers, creepers and trees. Its splendour is equal to that of millions of suns, and it is ever free from the six waves of material nature (namely, lust, anger, greed, envy, false ego and illusion).

content.content: Meditation on Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa

content.sanskrit:
dīvyad-vṛndāraṇya-kalpa-drumādhaḥ
śrīmad-ratnāgāra-siṁhāsana-sthau
śrī-śrī-rādhā-śrīla-govinda-devau
preṣṭhālībhiḥ sevyamānau smarāmi
content.translation: In the shining land of Śrī Vṛndāvana, in a temple composed of jewels, Śrī Rādhā-Govinda are seated on an effulgent throne beneath a kalpa-vṛkṣa tree, accepting service from Their beloved sakhīs. I meditate upon Them. [Śrī Caitanya-caritāmṛta Ādi-līlā 1.16]

content.sanskrit:
sat-puṇḍarīka-nayanaṁ meghābhaṁ vaidyutāmbaram
dvi-bhujaṁ veṇu-vaktrābjaṁ vana-mālinam īśvaram
divyālaṅkāraṇopetaṁ sakhībhiḥ pariveṣṭitam
cid-ānanda-ghanaṁ kṛṣṇaṁ rādhāliṅgita-vigraham
content.translation: I meditate on Śrī Kṛṣṇa (seated on the jewelled throne), who possesses beautiful lotus eyes, whose bodily hue resembles that of a fresh raincloud, who wears a yellow silken dhotī, who has two arms, who plays the flute, who wears beautiful forest garlands, who is adorned with glittering ornaments, who is surrounded by the gopīs, who embraces Śrīmatī Rādhikā and whose body is composed of transcendental consciousness and bliss.

content.sanskrit:
śrī kṛṣṇaṁ śrī-ghana-śyāmaṁ
pūrṇānanda-kalevaram
dvi-bhujaṁ sarva-deveśaṁ
rādhāliṅgita-vigraham
content.translation: I meditate on Śrī Kṛṣṇa, whose bluish-black colour resembles that of a fresh raincloud, whose body is full of transcendental bliss, who has a two-armed form, who is the Lord of all the demigods and who is embraced by Śrīmatī Rādhikā.

content.content: After meditating in this way, chant Sri Gāndhārvikā-Giridhārī Sri Rādhā-Vinoda-Bihārījī kī jaya. First perform worship within the mind (mānasa-pūjā), and then, with external items, perform worship of the Divine Couple -- in the form of the Deity. Offer each item first to Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa, then Śrīmatī Rādhārānī.
Reception
While ringing a bell, chant:

content.sanskrit: eṣa puṣpāñjali klīng gaurāya svāhā śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām namaḥ

content.content: Offer flower petals to Their Lordship's lotus feet.

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ āsanaṁ klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ

content.content: Offer an āsana for Their Lordships to sit on. Escort Them to the place of worship with a gesture of your hands. Offer a flower dipped in candana into the snāna-pātra for the purpose of offering an āsana.

content.sanskrit:
svāgataṁ su-svāgatam klīng gaurāya svāhā śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām
namaḥ

content.content: With joined palms welcome Their Lordships and see to Their comfort.

content.sanskrit:
etat pādyaṁ klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ

content.content: Offer padya water to wash Their Lordships' lotus feet.

content.sanskrit: idaṁ arghyam klīng gaurāya svāhā śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām namaḥ

content.content: Offer arghya to Their Lordships in Their hands so that They can sprinkle it over Their heads.

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ ācamanīyaṁ klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ

content.content: Offer Their Lordships water for sipping.

content.sanskrit: eṣa madhuparkaḥ klīng gaurāya svāhā śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām namaḥ

content.content: Offer cups of madhuparka to Their Lordships.

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ punar-ācamanīyaṁ klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām namaḥ

content.content: Again, offer water for sipping.
Bathing and Dressing

Mūrti-śuddhi: carefully remove the Deities' clothes and ornaments being careful not to scratch or harm Them in any way. Do not drag the clothes across Their faces. Cleanse Their Lordships with a soft cloth or sponge dampened with warm water, wiping off the candana, tulasī leaves, and so on.

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ sugandha-tailam klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām namaḥ

content.content: Massage Their Lordships with fragrant oil.

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ snānīyam klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām namaḥ

content.content: Bathe Their Lordships by pouring water from the conch (filling it at least three times with water) while ringing the bell and chanting the Brahma-saṁhitā and other appropriate prayers.

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ aṅga-vastram klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām namaḥ

content.content: Dry Their Lordships with soft cloths.

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ ācamanīyaṁ klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ

content.content: Offer Their Lordships water for sipping.

content.sanskrit: idaṁ vastram klīng gaurāya svāhā śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām namaḥ

content.content: Offer fresh clothing to Their Lordships. (dhoti)

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ ācamanīyaṁ klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām namaḥ

content.content: Offer Their Lordships water for sipping.

content.sanskrit: idaṁ upavītam klīng gaurāya svāhā klīṁ kṛṣṇāya namaḥ

content.content: Offer upavīta to Lord Caitanya and Sri Kṛṣṇa.

content.sanskrit: idaṁ tilakam klīng gaurāya svāhā klīṁ kṛṣṇāya namaḥ

content.content: Offer ūrdhva-puṇḍra tilaka to Lord Caitanya and Sri Kṛṣṇa before putting on upper cloth.
Note: When offering tilaka you may also chant the names of Viṣṇu, as when applying tilaka on yourself: oṁ keśavāya namaḥ for the forehead, etc.

content.sanskrit: imāni ābharaṇāni klīng gaurāya svāhā śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām namaḥ

content.content: Offer Their Lordships ornaments and decorate Them.

content.sanskrit: idaṁ malyam klīng gaurāya svāhā śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām namaḥ

content.content: Offer flower garlands.

content.sanskrit: idaṁ kaṅkaṇam śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām namaḥ

content.content: Comb, arrange, and decorate Their hair.
Worship

content.sanskrit: eṣa gandhaḥ klīng gaurāya svāhā śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām namaḥ

content.content: Apply candana mixed with scents, according to season, to Their Lordships' lotus feet, hands and head.

content.sanskrit: etāni puṣpāni klīng gaurāya svāhā śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām namaḥ

content.content: Offer fragrant flowers or flower petals dipped in candana to Their Lordships' lotus feet while ringing a bell. You may offer additional flowers for decoration at this time.

content.sanskrit:
etat tulasī-pātram (for one leaf) or etāni tulasī-patrāṇi (for several
leaves) klīṁ kṛṣṇāya namaḥ

content.content: Offer tulasī leaves and mañjarīs with candana to Kṛṣṇa's lotus feet. Place a tulasī leaf in Rādhā's right hand, for Her to offer to Kṛṣṇa.

content.sanskrit: eṣa dhūpaḥ klīng gaurāya svāhā śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām namaḥ

content.content: Offer incense.

content.sanskrit: eṣa dīpaḥ klīng gaurāya svāhā śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyām namaḥ

content.content: Offer a ghee lamp.
See "Offering bhoga" section.
Offer respect, glorification and obeisances. Silently chant the gopāla-mantra and the kāma-gāyatrī ten times each, and then chant the rādhā-gāyatrī ten times.
Śrī rādhā-mantra

content.sanskrit: rāṁ rādhāyai namaḥ

content.content: Śrī rādhā-gāyatrī

content.sanskrit: rāṁ rādhikāyai vidmahe prema-rūpāyai dhīmahi tan no rādhā praco-dayāt

he kṛṣṇa karuṇā-sindho dīna-bandho jagat-pate
gopeśa gopikā-kānta rādhā-kānta namo 'stu te
content.translation: I offer praṇāma to Śrī Kṛṣṇa, who is an ocean of mercy, the friend of the distressed and the source of creation. He is the master of the gopas, and the lover of the gopīs, headed by Śrīmatī Rādhikā.

content.sanskrit:
tapta-kāṣcana-gaurāṅgi
rādhe vṛndāvaneśvari
vṛṣabhānu-sute devi
praṇamāmi hari-priye
content.translation: I offer praṇāma to Śrīmatī Rādhikā, whose complexion is like molten gold, and who is the queen of Vṛndāvana. She is the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu mahārāja and is very dear to Śrī Hari.

content.sanskrit:
mahābhāva-svarūpā tvaṁ
kṛṣṇa-priyā-varīyasi
prema-bhakti-prade devi
rādhike tvāṁ namāmy aham
content.translation: O Śrīmatī Rādhikā, because You are the embodiment of mahābhāva, You are Śrī Kṛṣṇa's most dear beloved. O goddess, You alone can bestow pure love for Śrī Kṛṣṇa. Therefore, I offer my humble obeisances unto You.

content.content: After the worship of the Deity is complete, offer the mahā-prasāda and any prasādī (remnant) flowers or garlands to śrī guru and the Vaiṣṇavas by chanting the following mantras:

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ mahā-prasādaṁ nirmālyādikam aing śrī gurave namaḥ
idaṁ pānīyaṁ aing śrī gurave namaḥ
idaṁ ācamanīyaṁ aing śrī gurave namaḥ
idaṁ sarvaṁ oṁ sarva-sakhībhyo namaḥ
idaṁ sarvaṁ oṁ sarva-vaiṣṇavebhyo namaḥ
idaṁ sarvaṁ oṁ śrī paurṇamāsyai namaḥ
idaṁ sarvaṁ oṁ sarva-vraja-vāsibhyo namaḥ

content.content: Chant the aparādha-śodhana mantras (Prayers Begging Forgiveness for Offences).
Keeping this conception within the heart, one should worship śrī gurudeva according to one's capacity, with sixteen articles.

Śrī Gaurasundara is sitting on a jewel-studded altar at the Yogapīṭha in Māyāpura, which is part of the transcendental realm of Śrī Navadvīpa-dhāma. Śrī Nityānanda Prabhu is standing to His right side, Śrī Gadādhara to His left, Śrī Advaita Prabhu is standing before Him with folded hands, and Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita is standing behind Him, holding an umbrella. [Just below the jewelled altar of Śrī Gaurasundara, there is another altar, upon which śrī gurudeva is sitting.]
Offer praṇāma to the guru-paramparā, situated on the altar to the left of the deity, then recite:

content.sanskrit: aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: I offer obeisances to my spiritual master.

content.sanskrit: aing parama-gurave namaḥ
content.translation: I offer obeisances to my grand spiritual master.

content.sanskrit: aing parameṣṭhi gurave namaḥ
content.translation: I offer obeisances to my great grand spiritual master.

content.sanskrit: aing śrī guru-paramparāyai namaḥ
content.translation: I offer obeisances to śrī guru-paramparā.

content.sanskrit: oṁ sarva-gurūttamāya śrī kṛṣṇa-caitanyāya namaḥ
content.translation: I offer obeisances to the topmost of all gurus, Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya.

subsections.title: Mantra to meditate on Śrī Gurudeva

content.sanskrit:
prātaḥ śrīman navadvīpe
dvi-netraṁ dvi-bhujaṁ gurum
varābhaya-pradaṁ śāntaṁ
smaret tan nāma-pūrvakam
content.translation: In the early morning, chant śrī gurudeva's name while remembering him as being situated in Śrī Navadvīpa or Śrī Vṛndāvana-dhāma, and as possessing two eyes and two arms. He bestows fearlessness and is the embodiment of peacefulness.

content.content: After reciting this mantra, say the name of one's own śrī gurudeva three times, saying 'jaya!' after each time. First worship śrī gurudeva within the mind (mānasa-pūjā). Then, pray for his mercy and worship him with the external items, while reciting the guru-mantra received at the time of dīkṣā.
While offering the following articles, meditate that, having called upon śrī gurudeva to sit in the snāna-pātra, one is bathing him.

content.sanskrit: idaṁ āsanaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: (1) āsana (seat) — Offer a flower dipped in candana into the snāna-pātra.

content.sanskrit: prabho! kṛpayā svāgataṁ kuru aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: (2) svāgata (a welcome) — With folded hands, invite śrī gurudeva to sit on the āsana situated in the snāna-pātra.

content.sanskrit: etat pādyaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: (3) pādya (footbath) — Offer water by means of a blade of kuśa grass [or with the pañca-pātra] at his lotus feet, into the snāna-pātra.

content.sanskrit: idaṁ arghyaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: (4) arghya — Offer arghya (candana, flowers and water) into the snāna-pātra.

content.sanskrit: idaṁ ācamanīyaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: (5) ācamanīya (mouthwash) — Offer water into the throw-out pot.

content.sanskrit: eṣa madhuparkaḥ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: (6) madhuparka — Offer madhuparka into the snāna-pātra.

content.sanskrit: idaṁ punar ācamanīyaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: Offer water into the throw-out pot.

content.sanskrit: idaṁ snānīyaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: (7) snāna (bath) — Bathe śrī gurudeva with fragrant water poured from the conch shell into the snāna-pātra.

content.content: Note: One can dry the limbs of śrī gurudeva by mind, or wave a cloth in front of him in ārati fashion.

content.sanskrit: idaṁ sottarīya-vastraṁ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: (8) vastra (cloth) — Offer two flowers into the throw-out pot for the purpose of offering an upper and lower garment.

content.sanskrit: idaṁ ācamanīyaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: Offer water into the throw-out pot.

content.content: After this, meditate upon śrī gurudeva sitting at his place on the altar. Touch his feet and chant the guru-mantra eight times. This is called prasādhana [meaning that the bathing of śrī gurudeva and offering cloth to him is complete].

content.sanskrit: idaṁ upavītaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: (9) upavīta (sacred thread) — Offer the sacred thread (upavīta) to śrī gurudeva, or in its absence, a flower.

content.sanskrit: idaṁ tilakaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: Draw the tilaka mark on śrī gurudeva [or meditate on doing so].

content.sanskrit: idaṁ ābharaṇaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: (10) bhūṣaṇa (ornaments) — Offer a flower for decorating with ornaments.

content.sanskrit: eṣa gandhaḥ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: (11) gandha — Offer gandha at the lotus feet of śrī gurudeva.

content.sanskrit: idaṁ sagandhaṁ puṣpaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: (12) puṣpa (flowers) — Offer a flower dipped in candana at his lotus feet.

content.sanskrit: etat tulasī patraṁ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: Place tulasī in his right hand. [If necessary, use candana to make it adhere.]

content.sanskrit: eṣa dhūpaḥ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: (13) dhūpa (incense) — Offer water to signify incense.

content.sanskrit: eṣa dīpaḥ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: (14) dīpa (ghee lamp) — Offer water to signify a ghee lamp.

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ naivedyaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
idaṁ pānīyaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
idaṁ punar ācamanīyaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: (15) naivedya (foodstuffs) — Offer foodstuffs, water, and ācamana.

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ tāmbūlaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
idaṁ mālyaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
idaṁ sarvaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
content.translation: (16) mālya (garland) — Offer tāmbūla, garland, and all remaining items.

content.content: After this, chant guru-gāyatrī ten times and offer him prayers and obeisances.

content.sanskrit:
tvaṁ gopikā vṛṣa-raves tanayāntike 'si
sevādhikāriṇi guro nija-pāda-padme
dāsyaṁ pradāya kuru māṁ vraja-kānane
śrī rādhāṅghri-sevana-rase sukhinīṁ sukhābdhau
content.translation: O Gurudeva! You are immersed in an ocean of bliss and you can award transcendental service. You are a dear most gopikā of Śrīmatī Rādhikā. Please bestow upon me the shelter of your lotus feet, so that I can be blissfully engaged in the transcendental service of the Queen of Vraja, Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī.

content.content: Obeisances to Śrī Gurudeva

content.sanskrit:
oṁ ajñāna-timirāndhasya jñānāñjana-śalākayā
cakṣur unmīlitaṁ yena tasmai śrī-gurave namaḥ
content.translation: I offer my most humble daṇḍavat-praṇāma to śrī guru, who has opened my eyes, blinded by the darkness of ignorance, with the torchlight of knowledge.

content.sanskrit:
rādhā-sammukha-saṁsaktiṁ
sakhī-saṅga-nivāsinīm
tvām ahaṁ satataṁ vande
mādhavāśraya-vigraham
content.translation: O spiritual master, I continually offer my obeisances unto you, who are always in the presence of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī and very much devoted to Her. You always reside in the association of Her confidantes, the gopīs, and you are the abode of loving devotion to Śrī Mādhava.

content.sanskrit:
nāma-śreṣṭhaṁ manum api śacī-putram atra svarūpaṁ
rūpaṁ tasyāgrajam uru-purīṁ māthurīṁ goṣṭhavāṭīm
rādhā-kuṇḍaṁ giri-varam aho rādhikā-mādhavāśāṁ
prāpto yasya prathita-kṛpayā śrī-guruṁ taṁ nato 'smi
content.translation: I bow down to the beautiful lotus feet of my spiritual master, by whose causeless mercy I have obtained the supreme holy name, the divine mantra; the service of the son of Sacīmātā; the association of Śrīla Svarūpa Dāmodara, Rūpa Gosvāmī, and his older brother, Sanātana Gosvāmī; the supreme abode of Mathurā; the blissful abode of Vṛndāvana; the divine Rādhā-kuṇḍa and Govardhana Hill; and the desire within my heart for the loving service of Śrī Rādhikā and Mādhava in Vṛndāvana.

content.content: Obeisances to the Vaiṣṇavas

content.sanskrit:
vāñchā-kalpa-tarubhyaś ca kṛpā-sindhubhya eva ca
patitānāṁ pāvanebhyo vaiṣṇavebhyo namo namaḥ
content.translation: I offer praṇāma to the Vaiṣṇavas, who, just like desire trees, can fulfil the desires of everyone and who are full of compassion for the conditioned souls.

content.content: Mahā-mantra

content.sanskrit:
hare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare hare
hare rāma hare rāma rāma rāma hare hare

content.content: The procedure for offering foodstuffs (bhoga) is the same throughout the day.

The devotee should consider himself fallen and unworthy, not qualified to offer bhoga to the Lord, but should pray to Gurudev, knowing that he is a worthy servant. One should offer asana to Gurudev and call out to him: "Please offer this bhoga to the Deities you worship." (We ask the Guru to offer food to Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa, but we do not offer it for him to eat).

Although the guru offers food, the disciple, while chanting the mantras, meditates on being helped by him. The gaura-mula-mantra is always chanted along with the mula-mantra of Śrī Śrī Rādhā Kṛṣṇa, even when the vigraha of Lord Caitanya is not present.
We should offer the bhoga with a feeling that śrī gurudeva himself is offering the bhoga to Śrīman Mahāprabhu and Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa.
First purify yourself by performing ācaman by reciting the following mantras:

content.sanskrit: oṁ keśavāya namaḥ
oṁ nārāyaṇāya namaḥ
oṁ mādhavāya namaḥ

content.content: Make bhoga plates.
Close Deity curtains, wipe tables with wet cloth.
Clean floor.
Remove crown and flute, place asanas, put flower petals on them.
Bring in offering plates.
While ringing the bell and chanting the following mantras, invite Deities to āsanaṁ. Offer puṣpāñjali (a few flower petals) at the lotus feet of each deity:

content.sanskrit:
eṣa puṣpāñjaliḥ klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ

idaṁ āsanaṁ klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ
content.translation: Offer āsana (a seat).

content.sanskrit:
etat pādyaṁ klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ
content.translation: Offer pādya (washing the lotus feet).

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ ācamanīyaṁ klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ
content.translation: Offer ācamana (sipping water).

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ naivedyaṁ sa tulasī pānīya jalam klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ
content.translation: Offer naivedya with tulasī and water.

content.content: Place a Tulasī leaf on each dish offered. Hold the right hand over the bhoga plate, place the left hand on top of the right hand and repeat the gopāla-mantra 8 times.
Come out of the Deity room. Chant the gaura-gāyatrī ten times as well as the kāma-gāyatrī ten times and wait for some time for the Lord to take His meal.
After some time pujari knocks on the door and enters the Deity room. Invite the Deities to rinse their mouths with the following mantras:

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ ācamanīyaṁ klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ
content.translation: Offer mouthwash.

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ tāmbūlaṁ klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ
content.translation: Offer tambula (betel).

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ āsanaṁ klīng gaurāya svāhā
śrīng klīng rādhā-kṛṣṇābhyāṁ namaḥ
content.translation: Offer āsana (a seat).

subsections.title: Offer mahā-prasād to Gurudev and others

content.sanskrit:
idaṁ mahā-prasādaṁ aing gurave namaḥ
idaṁ mahā-prasādaṁ oṁ sarva sakhibhiyo namaḥ
idaṁ mahā-prasādaṁ oṁ vrindayai namaḥ
idaṁ mahā-prasādaṁ oṁ paurnamasyai namaḥ
idaṁ mahā-prasādaṁ oṁ sarva vaiṣṇavebhyo namaḥ
idaṁ mahā-prasādaṁ oṁ sarva vaiṣṇavībhyo namaḥ
idaṁ mahā-prasādaṁ oṁ sarva vrajavāsībhyo namaḥ

content.content: Then at the end, after the offering, we meditate that your spiritual master, where appropriate, are taking the remnants both in Kṛṣṇa-līlā and Gaura-līlā after all the associates have taken.
After the food offering, while singing the Holy name of the Lord in a mood of servitude, clean the Deity room with water. Then clean the paraphernalia used in the worship of the Lord.
After the bhoga offering, place the crown and flute back on the Deities, open Deity curtains and perform ārati.
Note: It should be understood that by the mercy of the spiritual master, we are able to engage in the service of the Lord in His Arcā-vigraha, like offering bhoga.
Other variations

idaṁ naivedyaṁ
idaṁ pānīya-jalam
idaṁ anna-vyañjana-pānīyādikaṁ sarvaṁ
idaṁ miṣṭānna-pānīyādikam

Sanskrit term Caraṇāmṛta meaning "the nectar of the feet." It refers to the sacred water or liquid that has been used to wash the feet of a deity, spiritual master, or saint. It is considered highly purifying and spiritually potent, imbued with divine blessings.

content.sanskrit:
akāla-mṛtyu-haraṇaṁ
sarva-vyādhi-vināśanam
viṣṇoḥ pādodakaṁ pītvā
śirasā dhārayāmy aham
content.translation: I drink the water from the lotus feet of Śrī Viṣṇu, which destroys all diseases and removes the chance of untimely death, and I also take that water on my head.

subsections.title: Mantra for honouring the caraṇāmṛta of Śrī Gurudeva

content.sanskrit:
aśeṣa-kleśa-niḥśeṣa-kāraṇaṁ śuddha-bhakti-dam
guroḥ pādodakaṁ pītvā
śirasā dhārayāmy aham
content.translation: I drink the water from the lotus feet of śrī gurudeva, which bestows pure bhakti and which causes the destruction of unlimited miseries and pains. I also take that water on my head.

subsections.title: Mantra for honouring the caraṇāmṛta of Śrī Gaurāṅgadeva

content.sanskrit:
aśeṣa-kleśa-niḥśeṣa-kāraṇaṁ śuddha-bhakti-dam
gaura-pādodakaṁ pītvā
śirasā dhārayāmy aham
content.translation: I drink the water from the lotus feet of Śrī Gaurasundara, which bestows pure bhakti and which causes the destruction of unlimited miseries and pains. I also take that water on my head.

subsections.title: Mantra for honouring the caraṇāmṛta of Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa

content.sanskrit:
śrī-rādhā-kṛṣṇa-pādodakaṁ
prema-bhakti-daṁ mudā
bhakti-bhareṇa vai pītvā
śirasā dhārayāmy aham
content.translation: The water from the lotus feet of Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa is the source of pure bhakti to Them. Having drunk that water with great joy and devotion, I also take that water on my head.

content.content: Before the abhisheka begins, ask the assembled devotees to chant Kirtan, warn Kirtaniyas that during the beginning and end of the abhisheka the Kirtan should be loud.

Prepare all paraphernalia (wash and dry beforehand): Jugs for liquids; Large container for charinamrita; Table for ablution, Snana Vedi; Deity stand; Conch shells for bathing; Dowel, Sahasra-dhara; Fruit garlands; Stands and mats; Paper napkins for hands; Ācamana, chamara and bell; Tray with 9 wicks and 9 bases (made of dough - flour, milk, sugar) arranged in lotus shape decorated with flowers, Pishta-pradipa; A mixture of dried banana powders (1), rice flour (1), flour (0.5), barley flour (1) urad dal flour (1) and kunkum, sindur (1); Purification brush; A bowl of fresh Tulasī leaves; Cup with sandalwood paste; Petals of 5 kinds of flowers, Pushpa.

Prepare the liquids: Madhuparka in a cup with a spoon; Fragrant oil, Tailam; A mixture of ground sesame (sesame) with water, Tila-udvartanam; Boil milk (3 liters) and let it cool down; Yogurt (3 liters) at room temperature, whisk to a smooth liquid; Ghee butter (50 mm), melted; Honey (0.5 L), melted, can be mixed a little with water; Sugar syrup (2-3 L, boil 1 liter of water / 18 scoops of sugar) at room temperature; Sweet juices (3-4 liters); Water (5-6 liters) at room temperature; Coconut water (or fresh coconuts); Rose water (1 liter).
Add tulasī to all the ingredients for abhisheka. In the pujari room (behind the closed curtains of the altar), change the Deities into gamchas and put on day garlands.
Purification of the paraphernalia (dravya-śuddhi): Lightly sprinkle water from the ācaman on the paraphernalia and repeat: oṁ astrāya phaṭ

Preparation of Snana vedi: Wash and decorate the Snāna vedi, the place of bathing well with cloths, fruits and flowers. Draw a Goloka yantra at the place where the Deities will be bathed and offer flowers and Tulasī to the place where the Deities will stand.
Bell worship (ghanta-puja)
Offer flower petals with sandalwood paste to the bell ringer and repeat:

content.sanskrit: ete gandha-puspe oṁ jaya-dhuani-mantra-matāh svāhā

content.content: Attach the flower petal to the body of the bell with a chandana. Then ring the bell lightly with the left hand and place it on the plate, while thinking of the bell as a servant of the Lord.
Worship of the Conch shell (sarkha-puja)
Offer flower petals with sandalwood paste to the shell and repeat:

content.sanskrit:
ete gandha-puspe
oṁ hum hum hum namah maha-sankhaya svāhā

content.content: Open the curtains of the altar, ringing the bell and trumpeting the conch shells, escort the Deities to Snana vedi. Offer: esa puspanjalih..., idaṁ āsanaṁ..., etat pādyaṁ..., idaṁ arghyam..., idaṁ acamaniyam..., esa madhuparkah..., idaṁ punar-acamaniya...
Welcome arati - Pista dipa: By ringing the bell and trumpeting the conch shells, offer Pista-pradipah to the Deities.

Massage: Remove the flower garlands from the Deities. Rub the Deities with oil (idaṁ sugandha-tailam...). Then rub the Deities with sesame paste 3/4 (idaṁ tila-udvartanam...). Wash the Deities with water and sesame paste through the sink 1/4 (idaṁ til-snaniyam...). Offer fruit garlands to the Deities (idaṁ phala-malayam...).
Panchamrta snana: Milk (idaṁ ksira-snaniyam...), Yogurt (idaṁ dadhi-snaniyam...), Ghee (idaṁ ghrta-snaniyam...), Honey (idaṁ madhu-snaniyam...), Syrup (idaṁ sita-snaniyam...).
Powders: Using a cow's tail as a brush, apply a mixture of powders on the Deities. Clean the Deities with a brush. Wash the Deities with plain water (idaṁ jala-snaniyam...).
Phalam: Bathe the Deities with fruit juices (preferably 5 sweet kinds) (idaṁ phala-snaniyam...).
Sugar powder: Using a cow's tail as a brush, apply sugar powder on the Deities. Clean with a brush. Wash with plain water (idaṁ jala-snaniyam...).
Gandha: Cover the Lord's body with Sandal paste (idaṁ gandha...). Bathe with water diluted with sandal paste (idaṁ gandha-snaniyam...).
Sahasrada dhar Showers: Offer pure water through a shower (idaṁ sahasrada-snaniyam...).

Concluding activities: Wipe the Deities with towels and wrap Them (idaṁ anga-vastram...). Sprinkle with petals from 5 kinds of flowers (idaṁ puspa-snaniyam...). Offer ācaman (idaṁ acamaniyam...). By ringing the bell and blowing the conch shells, escort the Deities to the pujari room. Recite the mantras of forgiveness for offenses. Remove everything from the place of bathing. In Pujari room clean, bath and dress the Deities. Offer Bhoga plate and Arati to the Deities.

Gāndharvikā-Giridhārī-Rādhā-Vinoda-bihārījī kī jaya!
Oṁ viṣṇupāda aṣṭottara-śata śrī rūpanugācārya Śrī Prema Prayojana Prabhu
Nitya-līlā-praviṣṭa oṁ viṣṇupāda aṣṭottara-śata Śrī Śrīmad Bhaktivedānta Nārāyaṇa Gosvāmī Mahārāja kī jaya!
Nitya-līlā-praviṣṭa oṁ viṣṇupāda aṣṭottara-śata Śrī Śrīmad Bhaktivedānta Svāmī Mahārāja kī jaya!
Nitya-līlā-praviṣṭa oṁ viṣṇupāda aṣṭottara-śata Śrī Śrīmad Bhakti Prajñāna Keśava Gosvāmī Mahārāja kī jaya!
Nitya-līlā-praviṣṭa oṁ viṣṇupāda aṣṭottara-śata Śrī Śrīla Bhaktisiddhānta Sarasvatī Gosvāmī Prabhupāda kī jaya!
Nitya-līlā-praviṣṭa parama-bhāgavat-pravara Śrī Śrīla Gaurakiśora dāsa Bābāji Mahārāja kī jaya!
Nitya-līlā-praviṣṭa Śrī Śrīla Saccidānanda Bhaktivinoda Ṭhākura kī jaya!
Nitya-līlā-praviṣṭa vaiṣṇava-sārvabhauma Śrī Śrīla Jagannātha dāsa Bābājī Mahārāja kī jaya!
Śrī gauḍīya-vedāntācārya Śrī Śrīla Baladeva Vidyābhūṣaṇa Prabhu kī jaya!
Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī Ṭhākura kī jaya!
Śrīla Narottama, Śrīnivāsa, Śyāmānanda Prabhu-traya kī jaya!
Śrīla Kṛṣṇadāsa Kavirāja Gosvāmī Prabhu kī jaya!
Śrī Rūpa, Sanātana, Bhaṭṭa Raghunātha, Śrī Jīva, Gopāla-bhaṭṭa, dāsa Raghunātha ṣaḍ-gosvāmī-prabhu kī jaya!
Śrī Śvarūpa Dāmodara, Rāya Rāmānandādi Śrī Gaura-pārṣada-vṛnda kī jaya!
Nāmācārya Śrīla Haridāsa Ṭhākura kī jaya!
Prema se kaho Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya, Prabhu Nityānanda, Śrī Advaita, Gadādhara, Śrīvāsādi Gaura-bhakta-vṛnda kī jaya!
Śrī Antardvīpa Māyāpura, Sīmantadvīpa, Godrumadvīpa, Madhyadvīpa, Koladvīpa, Ṛtudvīpa, Jahnudvīpa, Modradrumadvīpa, Rudradvīpātmaka Śrī Navadvīpa-dhāma kī jaya!
Śrī Śrī Rādhā-Kṛṣṇa gopa, gopī, go, Govardhana, dvādaśa vanātmaka Śrī Vraja-maṇḍala kī jaya!
Śrī Rādhā-kuṇḍa, Śyāma-kuṇḍa, Gaṅgā, Yamunā, Tulasī, Bhakti-devī kī jaya!
Śrīmatī Vṛndā-devī kī jaya!
Śrī Paurṇamāsī Yogamāyā kī jaya!
Śrī Gopīśvara Mahādeva kī jaya!
Śrī Jagannātha, Baladeva, Subhadrā, Sudarśana Cakra jiu kī jaya!
Śrī Kṣetra-maṇḍala kī jaya!
Sarva-vighna-vināśakārī Śrī Nṛsiṁhadeva kī jaya!
Bhakta-pravara Śrī Prahlāda Mahārāja kī jaya!
Cāri-dhāma kī jaya!
Cāri-sampradāya kī jaya!
Cāri-ācārya kī jaya!
Grantha-rāja Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam kī jaya!
Śrī Harināma-saṅkīrtana kī jaya!
Ananta-koṭi vaiṣṇava-vṛnda kī jaya!
Samāgata bhakta-vṛnda kī jaya!
Śrī Nitāi-Gaura-premānande! Hari Hari bol!

content.sanskrit:
oṁ mantra-hīnaṁ kriyā-hīnaṁ
bhakti-hīnaṁ janārdana
yat pūjitaṁ mayā deva
paripūrṇaṁ tad astu me
content.translation: O my Lord, O Janārdana, please allow whatever little pūjā I have performed to become complete, although I am devoid of devotion, unable to chant the mantras properly and imperfect in my performance.

content.sanskrit:
yad-dattaṁ bhakti-mātreṇa
patraṁ puṣpaṁ phalaṁ jalam
āveditaṁ nivedyan tu
tad gṛhāṇānukampayā
content.translation: Out of Your causeless mercy, please accept a leaf, flower, water, fruit, foodstuff or whatever has been offered with devotion.

content.sanskrit:
vidhi-hīnaṁ mantra-hīnaṁ
yat kiñcid upapāditam
kriyā-mantra-vihīnaṁ vā
tat sarvaṁ kṣantum arhasi
content.translation: If the mantras have been improperly chanted or the procedures have been imperfectly performed, please, kindly forgive me.

content.sanskrit:
ajñānād athavā jñānād
aśubhaṁ yan mayā kṛtam
kṣantum arhasi tat sarvaṁ
dāsyenaiva gṛhāṇa mām

sthitiḥ sevā gatir yātrā
smṛtiś cintā stutir vacaḥ
bhūyāt sarvātmanā viṣṇo
madīyaṁ tvayi ceṣṭitam
content.translation: Please forgive whatever inauspicious acts I have performed due to my being without proper knowledge or due to my performing them unknowingly and accept me as Your insignificant servant. Let my normal condition be service, let my movement be holy pilgrimage, let my thought be remembrance of You and let my words be glorification of You. O Viṣṇu, let the activities of my entire mind, body and soul, be engaged in You.

content.sanskrit:
aparādha-sahasrāṇi
kriyante 'har-niśaṁ mayā
dāso 'ham iti māṁ matvā
kṣamasva madhusūdana
content.translation: Thousands of offences are committed by me day and night. But think of me as Your servant and kindly forgive them, O Madhusūdana.

content.sanskrit:
pratijñā tava govinda
na me bhaktaḥ praṇaśyati
iti saṁsmṛtya saṁsmṛtya
prāṇān samdhārayāmy aham
content.translation: O Govinda, You promise that Your devotee will never perish. By remembering this over and over again, I am able to retain my life airs.

subsections.title: Short Version Begging Forgiveness for Offenses

content.content: aparādha-sodhana

content.sanskrit:
anga-hinam kriya-hinam
vidhi-hinam cayad bhavet
astu tat sarvam acchidram
krsna-karsna-prasadatah

yat kincid vaigunyam jatam
tad dosa-prasamanasya
sri-krsna-smaranam karomi
content.translation: May the mercy of Krsna and His devotees nullify all the mistakes we have made in our observance of the rules of worship and in our performance of the acts of worship. I now remember Krsna to nullify whatever faults there may be. [Sat-kriya-sara-dipika]

content.sanskrit:
hare kṛṣṇa hare kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa hare hare
hare rāma hare rāma rāma rāma hare hare

sections.subtitle: ṣoḍaśopacāra-pūjā

content.content:
pañca-pātra, containing fresh water and a spoon
bathing conch shell
a small bell on a plate
Dūpa, incense
Dīpa, ghee lamp
Visarjanīya-pātra, throw-out pot
Pūjā-pātra for the deity
Snāna-pātra bathing receptacle for the deity
Pūjā-pātra for śrī gurudeva
Snāna-pātra, bathing receptacle for śrī gurudeva
Water-pot

Blowing conch shell
āsana for the deity
āsana for the pujārī
If polishing metal Deities, powdered gopīcandana mixed with water
If marble Deities, soft cloth for washing
either cotton wool or a soft cloth for polishing
a soft cloth for wiping off the candana

a receptacle for caraṇāmṛta
a towel and gāmchā (cloth worn prior to dressing)
clothing, underclothing, and pins
ornaments and blutack
gandha (sandalwood paste)
flowers and flower garlands
tulasī leaves and mañjarīs (buds)

naivedya (fruits and/or sweets)
a picture of your spiritual master and a receptacle for offerings
Outside the Deity room, perform ācamana and offer obeisances to your spiritual master.

In the Deity room, clean the area where you will do the worship and place an āsana in position for performing the worship. While thinking of your spiritual master sitting on an āsana to perform the worship, sit on your own āsana.
Arranging the Paraphernalia for Worship (pātra-sthāpana)
Arrange the items in such a way that you can easily reach them without touching offered to un-offered items:

Place the bell on a plate to your left, and place the bathing conch on a stand so you can easily lift and replace it several times.
The waterpot, flowers, candana, and tulasī leaves should be within easy reach.
Use the throw-out pot (visarjanīya-pātra) for offered ācamana and arghya, or you may pour these liquids into the bathing receptacle (snāna-pātra) if it is a receptacle which has a drain. You may use a second visarjanīya-pātra for offered flower petals, cotton swabs, and so on.
Bathing towels should be placed on a tray, table or the altar, not on your lap or directly on the floor.
Purifying Hands (kara-śuddhi)
Wash your hands with water or smear them lightly with candana.
Purifying the Flowers (puṣpa-śuddhi)
Sprinkle the flowers with samānya-arghya water while chanting:

content.sanskrit: oṁ astrāya phaṭ

oṁ puṣpe puṣpe mahā-puṣpe
su-puṣpe puṣpa-sambhave
puṣpe cayavākīrṇe ca
huṁ phaṭ svāhā
content.translation: O flowers, O great and auspicious flowers who have appeared from a budding creeper, may you be purified.

content.content: Purifying the Lord’s Paraphernalia (dravya-śuddhi)
Sprinkle all the paraphernalia lightly with samānya-arghya water while chanting:

content.sanskrit: oṁ astrāya phaṭ

content.content: Purifying the Bodily Elements (bhūta-śuddhi)
Do prokṣaṇa by sprinkling yourself lightly with samānya-arghya water while chanting the Gopāla-mantra (the sixth guru-given mantra) once. Recite the following prayers, meditating on their meaning:

"By constitution I am an eternal servant of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, but by some misfortune, I became averse to Him since time immemorial. Having thus identified myself as a material body in this illusory existence and having been trapped in this repeated cycle of birth and death, I was burning in the blazing fire of the threefold miseries. However, by great fortune -- that is, by the mercy of śrī guru -- I have come to understand my identity as a minute, atomic spiritual particle, an eternal servant of Śrī Kṛṣṇa, completely aloof from the gross and subtle bodies. Now, by the order of śrī gurudeva, under his shelter, I have obtained the fortune of serving Śrī Guru-Gaurāṅga and Śrī Rādhā-Vinoda-bihārījī."
With this mood, while meditating on the soul within, recite the mantra for purification of oneself. (bhūta-śuddhi-mantra)

content.sanskrit:
nāhaṁ vipro na ca nara-patir nāpi vaiśyo na śūdro
nāhaṁ varṇī na ca gṛha-patir no vana-stho yatir vā
kintu prodyan-nikhila-paramānanda-pūrṇāmṛtābdher
gopī-bhartuḥ pada-kamālāyor dāsa-dāsānudāsaḥ
content.translation: "I am not a brāhmaṇa, kṣatriya, vaiśya or śūdra, nor am I a brahmacārī, gṛhastha, vānaprastha or sannyāsī. Being transcendental to this gross and subtle body, I am the servant of the servant of the servant of the lotus feet of the master of the gopīs, Śrī Kṛṣṇa, who is the ultimate shelter of everyone, full of transcendental bliss and an ocean of unlimited nectar." [Padyāvalī 74]

content.content: Meditation on the self (ātma-dhyāna-mantra)

content.sanskrit:
divyaṁ śrī-hari-mandirāḍhya-tilakaṁ kaṇṭhaṁ sumālānvitaṁ
vakṣaḥ śrī-harināma-varṇa-subhagaṁ śrī-khaṇḍa-liptaṁ punaḥ
pūtaṁ sūkṣmaṁ navāmbaraṁ vimalatāṁ nityaṁ vahantīṁ tanuṁ
dhyāyet śrī-guru-pāda-padma-nikaṭe sevotsukaṁ cātmanaḥ
content.translation: I meditate on myself as being surrendered at the lotus feet of śrī guru and possessing great determination to serve him. I meditate on myself, with my forehead adorned with beautiful tilaka, representing the temple of Śrī Hari. I am wearing a beautiful necklace of tulasī beads. My chest is marked with the holy name and anointed with sandalwood pulp. I am dressed in fine and pure cloth, and within, I am constantly absorbed in ever-fresh spiritual sentiments.

content.content: Note: In brief, before one begins arcana, one should clean the altar and the paraphernalia. Place the articles in a convenient place. It is important to sanctify the water in the pañca-pātra with the mantra "gaṅge ca yamune caiva..." and to purify flowers with the mantra "puṣpe puṣpe mahā-puṣpe...". Pādya, arghya and ācamanīya are usually substituted with pure water from the pañca-pātra. Similarly, if any item cannot be procured, it can be substituted by meditation and the offering of water from the pañca-pātra or conch shell.
